review_text: The ladies at the front desk are so kind, and the male workers is so friendly. The park is so clean; they do a wonderful job with the upkeep. Miss Birdie's takeaway is out front, so food is no problem for short stays. Kids will love the hot chips with chicken salt. They have a chicken & cockatoo enclosure for the kids. Wild ducks come to visit. They have on-site electric camping and beautiful bungalows, canvas tent-like accommodations you see on African safari holidays. God Bless you all.

First time here on a powered site (62) in a swag. Bench seating and reserve adjacent. Well spaced. Quiet.  Great location by the ocean and river. Sheltered from any big winds.  Camp kitchen well set up for the light traveler. Microwave, kettle, hot plate, cutlery, utensils. Lounge. TV. Handy if the weather is poor. As a female traveler I felt safe. Spotless amenities. Great hot shower. My group of 5 did the Kiama coastal walk over 2 days. Bus from outside holiday park to Werri beach starting point. A beautiful walk, but not one for summer. No shade. I used the pool when the 22km walk was done. Hired a fire pit for our stay. $60 with firefighters, poker, water bucket and  2 bags of wood. Lovely for the evening.
Such a great holiday park. The location is great, park facilities were spot on and the cabin we stayed in was brilliant.|Will definitely stay again ;)

It’s a great park overall and the tourist crowd definitely seem to enjoy it. The facilities are good and the location is convenient, however as a local, it wouldn’t personally be our first pick compared to some of the quieter parks along the South Coast.

The main thing to be aware of is the road noise. It can be quite busy and noisy during the day, although it does tend to quieten down at night. If you have young children, just keep in mind that the road can be dangerous, especially with the shop and jumping pillow both located across or near the main road.

The creek area is lovely at high tide and makes for a beautiful outlook, however access down to the water is quite steep and at low tide the water becomes quite brown/tan in colour and isn’t really swimmable. The beach can also get quite rough at times.

One tip — if you have a large caravan or bigger vehicle, definitely let reception know when booking. Some of the sites are very tight and can be difficult to manoeuvre into with larger setups.

Overall, it’s still a good park and we would stay again if other options were booked out.
Great location, well maintained, friendly staff

The staff are friendly, welcoming and accommodating. I stayed in the river side (Crooked River). There is a dump point. The pool area is lovely. The amenities were really clean. The powered sites themselves are a bit on the small side and the ground really uneven with quite deep hollows. It rains a lot in this area and my annexe floor filled up with water about 5cm deep in heavy rain. The sites need to be evened out to prevent this from happening. It spoiled the experience and puts me off staying there again. The phone reception is really poor, but there's nothing the park can do about that. Apparently the council won't allow Telstra to upgrade the towers to improve reception, which if true, is outrageous.

Great Park, We love the camp kitchen, the Amenities are great, Ezy wash in the laundry never works so I wouldn't even bother with it, get coins it will save so much Hassel.
Pet Owners not having their dogs on leads, thinking the world revolves around them, being rude and abusive when you kindly ask them to remove their pet from the pool area or put a lead on it. You are the kind of people who are going to ruin it for everyone else. This is one of the only parks on the south coast we could find that allow pets. AND FOR THIS REASON! I understand some pets have anxiety, some pets don't want to be away from their owners, but follow rules and don't ruin it for everyone else. You'd have to be a real clown to abuse another camper and get angry because YOU aren't following rules. They're there so everyone can have a good time. It would have been ok if your dog didn't bite the ankles of everyone who walks past it or your site. Clearly we can see where the dog gets its attitude from. Also, letting it chase the poor Bunnies here like it's a game at night time. As a dog owner and traveller I'd actually support no Pet Rules if they were to change. Because of people like you. Pretty disgusting that you allow your dog to chase wildlife and think it's ok.

It's pretty simple. If your dog has anxiety take it to the Vet and find out why get it treated like you would yourself. Absolutely disgusting pet owner 🤢

Great park, very friendly and approachable staff, also camp kitchen is awesome.
Only let down,  was pet owners not having pets on leash,  and taking into the pool area, sign say no pets in pool area and must be on leash at all times, most people are great and do the right thing, but some think it's there god given right, not to follow the rules, this is why parks are stopping pets from coming in
Great park for caravans to access beach and enjoy Berry. Was so convenient to get to Red Hot Summer tour event.

Spent 4 nights here in January 2026. Amenteties are dated and not terribly clean. Park is largely full of on site vans. Very pricey for the location and size. Wifi was very hit amd miss and mobile reception pretty weak as well. Not in a huery to go back. Local council run park and it shows!

We have been going to seven mile beach caravan park for 9 years now and it was the cleanest it has ever been. So many of the amenities have been fixed or replaced which was great to see after so many years of stuff broken and unusable.The staff were all lovely and easy to deal with. Made another fun year at seven mile beach caravan park so much better.

Under New Management
Improved conditions since last year. Great location with beach, river and pool options.
Camp kitchen upgraded and painted freshly.
New manager and his staff are friendly and always ready to assist.
We had an excellent short break here. Lovely pool. Ideal for families.
Management need to step up, toilets / showers were always full of sand ,bins overflowing, children running around screaming until late at night, was not enjoyable at all

I didn't book it. Good amenities. Great spot. Responded well to running out of toilet paper but realistically that shouldn't happen. Dbl bed in shack far softer than our preferance - might suit others.

Love this small well located caravan park.
Surf, beach walks, jumping pillow and pool we're all greatly appreciated. We especially appreciated the early check-in on Friday and 4pm checkout on Sunday. It made a full weekend. We plan on returning next year.
Had a look through here as it is close by to where we are staying. Looks a good place to stay and we will look to book in and try it out.

The owners of this beautiful park should be mighty proud. I watched their team work like clockwork to make EVERYTHING beautiful and my Grandaughter and I had a lovely relaxing few days. Definitely 2 nights not enough.
We will be saving our pennies to get back there from Wollongong ASAP. Far enough away to feel like a short drive to paradise.
I don't want to name anyone as I think the whole team, from top to bottom deserve a great big pat on the back.
I can't thank them all enough for a most relaxing and special time we shared.
A perfect place, one to visit as often as possible.
THANK YOU EVERYONE
Two very happy and spoilt customers 😀 😄
Great value for money, great facilities and just a minute walk to the beach
Love the safari tents and can't beat the location. There's a friendly cockatoo on site that brings the kids so much joy to talk to.

Great stay. Greeted at check-in by friendly staff, who continued to look after us throughout the week. Shout out to the team including Clinton, Stacey & Brett. Ensuite was spotlessly clean, large and well maintained. Our site was easy to navigate with our van. The park spans both sides of the road. Both sides have their own pools, playgrounds, BBQs and large camp kitchens. One side also has a kiosk, river and canoes and a talking cockatoo, the other has beach access, jumping pillow and a tennis court. Stacey suggested great local places to eat including takeaway or dine-in options. Grounds beautifully maintained, mown, nice garden beds. Large laundry. The atmosphere was friendly and peaceful, other guests were polite and respectful. Great clean beach with easy access. Dog friendly. Every day we had something fun to do including trips into Gerringong, Berry, Wollongong, Nowra and Kiama. There's so much here at the park and in the local area to keep everyone happy.  We've travelled Australia quite extensively over the years and we appreciate a good caravan park.  We've stayed at this park before and we'll definitely be back again. Big thanks to the management and staff.

Our family had the best weekend away at Seven Mile! We stayed in the safari tent right next to the river. We hired kayaks from the park and kayaked past our safari tent and also up the winding shady river bustling with birds and ducks. It was so peaceful and felt like we were in a world of our own. Highlights for the kids were also the talking cockatoo and jumping pillow. Highlights for the adults were the luxuries in the the safari tent we were not expecting (beautiful bathroom, deck with bbq, tv and kitchen) and also the short walk to the fisherman’s club for dinner. Our weekend was one of our favourite family holidays!
Stayed in the Beach Hut Cabin - great location, hut was clean and what was expected from a Holiday Park. Kids loves the jumping pillow and close to the beach.

We paid $795 for our off-season 2 night stay in the spa deluxe, and we were surprised by the lack of attention this cabin has received. From the descriptions on your website, we were led to believe we would be staying in a newly refurbished, high-quality cabin. While this cabin might not need extensive work, the small details matter greatly for an enjoyable experience.

Here are some specific concerns we encountered. Please note, these have been raised directly with Seven Mile Beach with no response.

1. Television and Streaming Services: We encountered difficulties accessing apps on the TV due to poor reception, even though we were signed into your Wi-Fi. Additionally, the absence of free in-house movies, which were advertised online, was disappointing.

2. Cabin Inventory: While we understand that breakages can happen, it would be helpful to conduct a thorough inventory check during the cabin cleaning process. We found:
- 3 champagne glasses
- 1 white wine glass only
- Insufficient cutlery (only 4 steak knives, 4 tea spoons, 6 knives/forks/spoons)
- no chopping boards
- 4 mismatched dinnerware sets making up just enough for 6 people

This impacted our stay, as we didn't have enough of the basics required to accommodate 5 people when using the kitchen facility to prepare meals.

3. Kitchen Appliances: The gas stove had two burners that weren’t functioning correctly, and the dishwasher handle would break off each time we attempted to slide open the drawer, which was quite inconvenient.

4. Kitchen Aesthetics: We noticed that one section of the kitchen was painted in blue, creating a mismatch in paint that detracted from the overall appearance.

5. Bathroom Issues: The bathroom urgently needs better ventilation; after one moderate temperature shower, everything became excessively wet. The flooring was also wet and slippery. We noticed this morning what appeared to be black mould growing on the ceiling near the shower head, which raises concerns regarding our health. It is essential to address any mould issues promptly, as they can pose health risks.

6. Shampoo Dispenser: We faced an unfortunate and disappointing situation when we discovered that the shampoo dispenser contained the same product as the hand soap, which led to significant tangles in both our daughters' long hair. We didn’t realise this until my husband washed his hair as well.

7. Air Conditioning Remote: The air conditioning remote did not fit its bracket correctly, and when we arrived, we almost stepped on the remote as it was on the ground near the entrance to the cabin.

8. BBQ Condition: The barbecue had a strong smell; upon investigation, my husband found a container of fat underneath, which held a substantial amount of grease. This not only poses a fire hazard but also attracts pests and creates unpleasant odors, impacting the overall experience.

9. BBQ Fridge Condition: The outdoor BBQ fridge had not been cleaned in a number of months, which further detracted from our experience.

We love Gerringing and will be back. However, we won't be staying at Seven Mile Beach holiday park.

So i have a caravan at this park me and my family quite enjoy it. It somewhat pet friendly and some might say that it is a drama free park which i sorta agree with but yesterday there was a major issue that started with kids and escalated to adults then the elderly and today my brother found a baby red belly black snake but it slithered under neeth a caravan so we told the office and they did absolutely nothing about it i just dont understand because i was going to bring my cats down but now do i not want to i also feel unsafe there with a venomus snake around me. The staff are some what okay I have seen better parks though and also tos is riverside but beach side is a million times better

I couldn’t recommend this park to anyone. The facilities were the filthiest I have experienced anywhere. It was obvious that they have never been  thoroughly cleaned   The floor was disgusting, mould in the shower, cobwebs everywhere. Filthy windows, a huge amount  bird droppings in the tiled beach shower area. Totally revolting.

Powered site backed straight onto the wildlife area and a few minutes walk to the beach! Absolutely awesome including the jumping pillow, swimming pool, playgrounds and the super friendly cockatoo and that live near reception!
Stayed at a powered caravan site has fantastic prices especially given the amenities around the park friendly staff and some adorable park pets from a talking cockatoo to rabbits and ducks

To start off, the staff were wonderful! Then we got to the "tent", exactly what we expected and more! Overlooking the little River, the tent was beautiful, rustic and inviting! I would highly recommend, if you wanted just to relax and unwind.
Beautiful location 2min walk to beautiful seven mile beach ⛱️ local possum visits thru the night

Stayed on the riverside caravan lots pretty run down old site zero internet, grey water straight into the creek, overgrown grass lots of dirt but cheap in price. The beachside look a million dollars compared to the riverside.

Seven Mile Holiday Park is in a beautiful location, and the staff are very friendly and helpful. However, compared to our last stay three years ago the park has gone down hill. The cabin was dirty when we checked in, and lacked the basics like garbage bags and washing sponges. Wifi is advertised however connection, when you could get it, was poor, the park should not advertise having wifi if it’s not readily accessible - perhaps put boosters around the site?. The sun chairs at the pool were broken. The shared toilet facilities were filthy and there was a lot of rubbish around the site. Security is an issue as the boom gates were not working. The list goes on….such a shame as it has the potential to be so much more with some care and investment.

The property was not as advertised, not as reviews indicate. Seven Mile Beach Holiday Park. I complained about the state of facility to the council who owns it, a representative came out and I endured an inspection highlighting all the issues. Overpriced and an allround very upsetting, disappointing experience and a terrible waste of my money.

Absolutely THE WORST ammenities in Australia. Staff attitude is an absolute disgrace. Zero care when brought to their attention. Yet my 11yo son stupidly jumped off some pool furniture into the pool and I’m threatened with being kicked out of the park. Double standards ever since this new care taker has moved in. Absolute bloody joke!

Kiama Council should be ashamed to run this holiday park.

We stayed on the Riverside and the lis of concerns is long! I believe the ocean side is not quite as bad.

The Council should not be taking people’s hard earned holiday money and not providing a safe and clean environment.

Some key items include:

* Disgraceful kitchen with a rancid non working oven. It is surely not a big thing to replace an oven and have basic maintenance. There was nothing in the kitchen in the way of cleaning items etc.
* Non functioning boom gate and basically no control over who entered the park creating parking issues
* Lack of adequate cleaning in bathrooms and replacement of bathroom items including paper towel. Hair and toilet paper stayed on the floor and in drains for days.
* Narrow, and unkempt campsites - the table at our friends site was broken and needed to be propped up to ensure safety to the kids
* I do not believe the bathrooms would pass code as my daughter got stuck in one and it is not possible to fit under or  over the door and no external access to the lock.
* Over the course of a week there was only one holiday activity for the children - we have stayed at many parks and have never not had school holiday activities. There’s just no effort from the park to create a great holiday experience.
^ Presence of a Christian Group which was very strange - they were running activities for kids which was very concerning.
* Ramshackle permanent area with extremely narrow roads. This whole area is an accident waiting to happen.
* Hole in the jumping pillow with no attempt to fix it for the duration of our stay.

It’s hard to pinpoint exactly what is going on with this park - clearly there are major issues. The whole park is unclean, poorly maintained and the riverside in particular is like being in an alternate universe. The Council needs to re-invest in this park and properly staff it.

I feel the staff onsite were trying their best but the parks needs capital investment.

The cafe appears to be permanently closed.

The only plus to this park is its beachside location.

Perfect location!!!
Friendly helpful staff
Beautiful pool beside the air-conditioned camp kitchen which has outdoor and indoor BBQs, pizza oven, fridges, TV and lounges.
Grounds are well kept and our caravan site had concrete slab and green mesh side by side. We had a lovely view of the river from our van and 1 minute walk down a designated track to the beach.
Dogs allowed except school holidays.
Highly recommend!!!

Our stay at this caravan park was fantastic! We booked a group of sites for our families and friends, all staying close together. The location itself is absolutely beautiful, offering a peaceful and scenic backdrop for our getaway.

The facilities within the park were top-notch. The swimming pool was a hit with the kids, and the jumping pillow provided endless fun for everyone.

The only downsides were the amenities block, which we found to be quite dirty during our visit, and my campsite, which was a bit of a dirt patch. The rain made it quite muddy, so keeping things tidy was a bit of a challenge. Despite this, it didn’t take away from the overall great experience.

We had a wonderful time and would recommend this caravan park for families and groups looking to spend quality time together. The beautiful location, excellent facilities, and family-friendly atmosphere make it a top choice for a fun and relaxing getaway!

Back for our yearly end of season trip and the caravan park is still a great pick. Just stroll across the road and the wonders of seven mile beach awaits you! The park has the cleanest facilities on a constant basis and that's down to the staff. Friendly, happy and very approachable and constantly checking that all is well, a real credit. The onsite pool is extremely popular and I might have a dip one day, but with Seven Mile Beach just a hop away, it is my first choice to get wet!

The riverside camping is pretty drab. The river is relatively stagnant, not suitable for swimming. The tent sites are extremely close to the road and are particularly noisy. The amenities were nothing to rave about.

Very disappointed with the park, has become very run down and the amenoties were disgusting, we even gave the feed back regarding them and they didnt care or do anything about them, cant see us going back, shame, as it used to be such a lovely little park

Ammenities are gross. Nothing is cleaned after staying for two nights. So disappointing as had been her many times through my childhood and early adult years and was amazing. Everything run down, including the no longer corner shop.
Absolutely cannot justify the $50 a night -

Booked a "riverside" powered site as we had Kyaks. River was a stagnant creek with no access and site was grass with huge dirt hole where the annex would have gone.
Go them to change to sea side (this meant crossing the public road). Site was ok .
Amenities were putrid. One bowl in the men's toilet was clogged and full. Stayed that way for nearly a day.
Whole place is messy and untidy and needs a good facelift and clean.
Not worth the $50. daily charge. Stayed in better $20. camp sites.
Worst "G'day park" I have stayed in.
We have a carevan here and what a great spot nice and quiet with a sort walk to the beach.

My girlfriends and I had an amazing weekend staying at this campsite! We had a beautiful view of a peaceful pond with ducks, and the whole place felt very family-friendly and safe. The highlight of our stay was definitely the lovely receptionist, Caira (sorry if that’s spelled wrong). She was an absolute gem, going above and beyond to help us find transportation to the closest winery. Her customer service was brilliant and truly made our stay memorable. Highly recommend this place for a relaxing getaway!

The caravan park was Amazing the location was incredible listening to the waves crashing as you fall asleep was beautiful. The park was quiet, and the amenities were clean and enjoyable, the pool was fun  as well . The Cockatoo was very Talkative and the beach was  Just across the Road .

Amazing stay, clean sites and bathroom, friendly staff, both a river and a beach access, pet friendly, this space is the whole package. Highly recommend anyone  Looking to have a getaway to come here for a short long-term. It is one of my most favourites Stays in New South Wales. 🩷

We had a site opposite the pool. Hardly anyone in the park with it being winter and out of school holidays. Biggest bonus was being able to hire a firepit and they supply wood (at a cost). Absolutely loved it here.

We recently stayed here at the end off our 10/000 km trip and must say the ammenities were absolutely disgraceful to the point we wouldn’t use them in the 2 days we stayed their they were not cleaned toilets were full off you know what the bin was overflowing with paper towels to the extent that they were on the ground most off the roadside drop toilets were better looked after
Lovely place to stay the rooms were nice and it’s in a lovely area perfect place to bring the kids 👍
Nice place to stay. Amenities were good and clean. Park was very tidy.  Couldn't take the dogs on the beach. That was disappointing.

Stayed at this dog friendly park with our dogs. Unfortunately the park is surrounded by bush, beach and a river, all where dogs are not allowed. Would have been best to tell us at the time of booking. Park was fully booked, mostly northern Sydney beaches teens, but nobody thought to have security on hand.
The park lacks maintenance in some areas and no shop. Would benefit from an experienced gardener.
I didn't stay in a cabin, so I can't comment.
The powered site has a concrete slab and meshed area, which was much better than just grass or dirt. These sites are a little tight in length if you have a long van and tow vehicle, but possible.
However, the toilets were clean, as was the pool, which was good to swim in.
I camped one night , great little place to stop and recover
Cabin/caravan in the park , great atmosphere, great for kids , close to the river and the beach , pub for a drink and Tucker is not far away.

Friday 16th Feb 2024
DO NOT GO TO THIS PARK
Rundown, uncared for, staff dishonest.
We booked a 'Glamping tent' what a joke it was moudly, dirty, smelt, cobwebs, dated furniture, full of mosquito's and NO net over the bed.
Location was next to a half dried up creek that smelt and is a breeding ground for the mosquito's.
Told that tent was pet friendly when we booked. Then when we called to pay in full on arrival were charged an extra $50 pet cleaning fee for a 3 yrs toy Poodle that has been fully house trained since he was 10wks old.

Very bad experience
Been coming to this holiday park for 27yrs
We was all set up for the week
After 3 nites , we was told to pack up and move to the site next to us
Absolute joke
We left , got refund for days remaining
Management are useless
The whole place looks run down
Use to be like a 5 star resort
Now 0/5 stars
The shop it closed
Tiles missing from pool
Hardly ever see any staff out working
The last management was hands on
Out and about do anything and everything
From sun up to sun down
We will never ever return to this
Caravan park ever again
Dont waste your money or time
Waren 2019 hier , waren auf nen Roadtrip von Sydney Richtung Adelaide für eine Nacht hier. Wunderschöner Platz

A warning to all - read this and other reviews and also take a look at the reviews on trip advisor as well. Not good. As a long-term regular customer, I have just had a really bad experience with this Holiday Park. Fully paid in advance but contracted COVID three days before due to attend in January Holidays. Did the right thing and didn't go and isolated at home. Kids really distort.  They refused to give any credit and stated I have lost all of my $735 even though they have a waiting list and had given the site to another party and charged them the same day! Extorsion!
I have had to take them to NSW Trading Standards and made a Formal Complaint to the Council who own the park.

We were on a site riverside and really enjoyed our stay here. Management and staff were friendly and  visibly active in maintaining the park & running activities. Amenities were clean and the animal farm was a great spot to visit.

Can we free the goats? I don't think it's fair to have those animals in the cages for the tiny amusement they bring the kids. Goats should be free roaming fields, standing on grass (not dirt), eating whatever they choose.
Absolutely amazing location with beautiful sights

If you stay on the riverside in the safari tents I give it 5stars all your amenities self contained with a great river view and a quiet location comphy bed late check out the deal breaker enjoy the serenity

My only advice is do not stay here. Manager and staff are extremely rude. Park is near nothing. Rooms are bare. They said pet friendly but they are not when you arrive and refuse to issue a refund. Management were also extremely rude to their own staff. Complete nightmare.
Stayed at seven mile holiday park for a university excursion, clean amenities and great location next to the beach. Staff were very friendly and helpful. Would recommend
Great place right by the water

I stayed in the basic Surf Shacks. There’s plenty of areas for cooking up. Even during the school holidays when the place was busy I never felt I had to wait to do my thing. With the beach a 3min walk away and even a nice bike/walk trail through forested area there was plenty to do if your just looking to chill and enjoy the outdoors… oh and the showers…. Probably the best, hottest full strength showers I’ve experienced staying anywhere!!
Amazing place to stay. Great customer service and facilities
Close to Gerroa and Kiama and Berry. Great park for kids.
Great place away with Dogs 🏞🐕❤️👌
